title: Iceberg out-of-bounds with access to older snapshot
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce.
### How to reproduce
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Int, c1 Int) ENGINE = IcebergLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/t0/');
INSERT INTO t0 VALUES (1,1),(2,2),(3,3);
ALTER TABLE t0 CLEAR COLUMN c0;
INSERT INTO t0 SETTINGS iceberg_snapshot_id = <snapshot id of the first insert>  VALUES (1,1),(2,2),(3,3); --Logical error
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace

poc: CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Int, c1 Int) ENGINE = IcebergLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/t0/');
ALTER TABLE t0 CLEAR COLUMN c0;
INSERT INTO t0 SETTINGS iceberg_snapshot_id = <snapshot id of the first insert>  VALUES (1,1),(2,2),(3,3);
key_bug_features: INSERT INTO with older iceberg_snapshot_id causes out-of-bounds error
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id Int32, name String, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now()) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
ALTER TABLE t1 ADD COLUMN age UInt8;
INSERT INTO t1 (id, name, age) VALUES (4, 'David', NULL), (5, 'Eve', 25);
INSERT INTO t1 SETTINGS iceberg_snapshot_id = 1 VALUES (6, 'Frank', 30);
